The Ather motor has made a revolutionary EV scooter with good design, with a wide seating space ensuring more comfort, and the under-seat space is also large, good to accommodate two helmets. It also has software, which additionally costs 14000rs. This software will give you the true navigational status of your scooter as well as the battery capacity. The rest of the features are not important or needed. This 14000rs also includes an extra battery warranty of 2 years. If you want to purchase a battery warranty for an extra 3 years, it will cost you more than 5000rs. This would give you 8 8-year battery warranty in total.Pros-Battery -This scooter has an efficient battery fully enclosed in a metal casing. It is unlike other scooters, which have unprotected batteries that get heated frequently, and battery capacity also deteriorates in other scooters.Body- The whole body is made of aluminium alloy, which makes it lighter and also increases the efficiency of the battery.Charging station - Ather is also a fast charging station. In my city, it has 5/6 charging stations at a range of 2km. They have a high charging grid in their service centre and outside their showroom. Now they provide free charging. In my city, it's free. Negative part is that it would charge your scooter upto 80%.Software- I liked the software system as it has a good navigational system and built-in GPS to track your scooter.It also provides an alert in case your scooter is shaken or moved by someone while it's locked in your mobile.Modes- It has two modes, eco and zip mode. On the highway, you canridein zip mode.Accessories - In the showroom, they will provide you free ISI-approved helmet. Rest, all things are chargeable.Additional guard and foot race will cost you 4000-5000 Rs, depending on the showroom.Service centre - Ather provides good and timely service, which you can book your slot from your mobile. Service charge is 600- 700 Rs, and additional prices depend on your scooter's condition and replaceable parts.Cons- Every scooter or bikes have pros and cons, but you will only hear the pros from the showroom personnel; they will never tell you the cons, you will only get cons after experiencing the scooter.I will provide the list of cons as I have found, as per my point of view-1st - In highway or for long-distancerides, the motor speed decreases -it means if youridefor a long time and you give constant acceleration, the speed gradually decreases.2nd- In the charging centre, the battery got charged within 45 minutes upto 80%, but at home it takes upto 3/5 hours, depending on the power supply.3rd - In my scooter's lock-in system for changing have some problems as it's locked sometimes, it won't.

Ather Rizta scooter looks very nice and is more comfortable than other electric scooters.I’ve beenridingthe Ather Rizta S 2.9 variant for several months now, and it is genuinely an incredible, family-focused electric scooter. If you are looking for a practical, comfortable, and reliable daily commuter, this base variant makes an absolute ton of sense for everyday cityriding. First off, the seating comfort is completely unmatched in this segment. The 900mm long seat easily accommodates two adults without ever feeling cramped. My pillion riders absolutely love the relaxed, upright posture. Furthermore, the 34-litre under-seat storage is a total lifesaver, easily holding my groceries and a full-face helmet. In terms ofperformance, the 2.9 kWh battery provides a realistic city range of about 80 km in Zip mode. While the Smart Eco mode gets me around 100 km/h. I prefer the responsive punch of Zip mode for daily traffic. The scooter handles smoothly, and the build quality is top-notch compared to rivals like the TVS iQube r/INDIANBIKES. However, it is not entirely flawless. The primary drawback is the charging time; a full charge using the standard charger takes about 8 hours, so you definitely need to plan your trips carefully. Additionally, I’ve noticed some mild squeaky belt noise, which is a common quirk in Ather scooters. It doesn’t affectperformance, but it can be a bit annoying. Despite these minor inconveniences, the Ather Rizta S 2.9 is a highly reliable, cost-effective option for the whole family. Theridequality is unbelievably smooth, and you never feel fatigued, even after long commutes. The peace of mind provided by the [8-year battery warranty].
